@nitesh\_kumar98 nice progress on this release. It feels like you focused on making everyday editing smoother instead of just adding flashy features, and I appreciate that.

I like that you focused on workflow instead of just adding flashy features. Keeping overlays and zooms synced after edits sounds like it'll save creators a lot of time.

The multi-clip recording alone makes this feel like a big step forward. Curious to see how people who record tutorials and demos find the new workflow.

Serand

Serand

I appreciate the attention to the little details too. Things like snapping, clean export cancellation, and recordings starting at the playhead make editing feel much smoother.

Report

Kate Sleeman

Hedy AI

Hedy AI [...] Plus polish: cancelling an export now stops cleanly, play resumes where you paused, and new recordings start with a gentler default zoom.

Still free for individuals, no watermark, runs on-device, notarized direct download for macOS 14+. I'd love to hear how the multi-clip flow feels. If it's useful to you and you'd like to chip in, there's a Ko-fi, but it's entirely optional!

Thank you all for the love and support for the product!

Congrats on the update. I really like that the facecam is now its own track because it gives much more flexibility during editing.

@nitesh\_kumar98 The on-device approach is a nice touch. I always prefer editing tools that keep my recordings local whenever possible

results.title: Glide Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
4.9

Based on 16 reviews

Review Glide?

Reviewers see Glide as a fast, approachable way to build and test apps without much coding, especially for MVPs, internal tools, and first projects. People repeatedly praise the spreadsheet-like workflow, simple interface, and quick feedback loop that helps small teams experiment and ship usable versions fast. Several say it is easy to pick up if you already know Excel or similar tools. The main caveat is scope: reviewers note limits around advanced features, deep customization, and more complex systems, with some frustration about free-plan and education-account restrictions.

Glide is a powerful app development platform that simplifies the process of creating functional and visually appealing applications. I enjoy exploring the features! With its drag-and-drop interface and pre-built components, building an app becomes accessible to users without coding experience. The platform offers a wide range of features and integrations, making it versatile for various app projects. However, some advanced customization options may be limited. Overall, Glide is an excellent choice for quickly prototyping and launching apps with ease. 👍👍

•7 reviews

#### What's great

I used Glide during college while experimenting with product ideas. I built a small dApp called dApp, loosely inspired by Bumble, to test an idea quickly.

Glide lets you turn spreadsheets into working apps with screens, logic, and actions. The feedback loop is fast.

I could prototype flows, change structure, and ship usable versions without getting blocked by code. That made experimentation addictive.

It is not meant for complex or deeply custom systems but for quick MVPs and early experimentation, Glide does a solid job of helping ideas come alive fast.

15 views

Glide CV

Glide CV

Maker [...] Report

Daryll Wong

Much needed for students like myself!! We need more easy, simple to use solutions like this! If there are plans to extend to other disciplines (aside from software engineering), it would be great as well. Update: Explored the product and wow, I really love the simplicity and transparency in showing us all that the ATS has learnt about myself, not just superficial scores and some comments, seen in other similar products. Might there also be "AI" to suggest better phrasing and automatically make my score better? Or "AI" to pick up certain areas and prompt me to include more information. Etc. These may take some work, but as of now, this product looks great! I will save it to my list of useful apps!

Report
